input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue with keyboard handling in .NET 10. This is a known change — in .NET 9+, `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed because Android itself deprecated the `adjustResize` soft input mode. The replacement in .NET MAUI is to use the new `KeyboardAcceleratorsChanged` or more specifically, the `IEdgeToEdge` / `AndroidX` approach with `WindowInsetsCompat`.

Let me think about this more carefully. In .NET MAUI (.NET 8+), there have been changes around how soft keyboard input is handled on Android. 

The old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach no longer works in newer versions because Android deprecated `adjustResize` in favor of the `WindowInsetsAnimation` API. For .NET 10, the recommended path is to use `WindowCompat.SetDecorFitsSystemWindows` and handle keyboard insets through `WindowInsetsCompat` instead.

In .NET MAUI specifically, the framework adopted edge-to-edge display by default on Android, which fundamentally changed how soft input modes are handled and likely broke the previous platform-specific configuration method.

The solution involves adapting to the new edge-to-edge model by either using `adjustPan` mode in the manifest, properly handling keyboard insets with the new APIs, or leveraging the `AndroidX WindowInsetsCompat` approach that respects the inset system.
